Scenario: Verify OPA installer downloads successfully from portal
Steps:
1. Log in to Cloudsfer
2. Navigate to OPA download page
3. Click Download

Scenario: Verify OPA installs successfully on Windows Server
Steps:
1. Run the downloaded installer on Windows Server
2. Follow installation wizard
3. Complete installation
ExpectedResult: OPA installed — service visible in Windows Services

Scenario: Verify OPA service starts automatically after install
Verify OPA registration with Cloudsfer account
Steps:
1. Open OPA configuration
2. Enter account token/credentials
3. Click Register
ExpectedResult: OPA registered — appears in portal under connected agents

Scenario: Verify OPA can be uninstalled cleanly
Steps:
1. Uninstall OPA via Windows Programs
2. Verify removal
ExpectedResult: Service removed — no leftover files or registry entries

Scenario: Verify OPA shows Online status in Cloudsfer portal
Steps:
1. Install and register OPA
2. Open portal > OPA section

Scenario: Verify OPA shows Offline when service is stopped
Steps:
1. Stop OPA service in Windows Services
2. Check portal

Scenario: Verify OPA version shown in portal matches installed version
Steps:
1. Check portal OPA status
2. Compare to installed version

Scenario: Verify OPA reconnects automatically after network interruption
Steps:
1. Disconnect network briefly
2. Reconnect
3. Check agent status
ExpectedResult: Agent reconnects and shows Online without manual restart

Scenario: Verify last seen timestamp updates regularly for Online agent
Steps:
1. Open portal OPA status
2. Wait 1–2 minutes
3. Refresh
ExpectedResult: Last seen timestamp updated — not stale

Scenario: Verify OPA can browse local file server folders
Steps:
1. Configure migration with OPA source
2. Browse local folder tree
ExpectedResult: Local file server folders visible and browsable

Scenario: Verify OPA migration from local file server to Cloudsfer destination
Steps:
1. Select local folder via OPA as source
2. Set Cloudsfer destination
3. Start migration
ExpectedResult: Files migrated from local server to destination

Scenario: Verify large files (1GB+) migrate correctly via OPA
Steps:
1. Include 1GB+ files in migration scope
2. Run migration
ExpectedResult: Large files transfer completely without corruption

Scenario: Verify file permissions preserved during OPA migration
Steps:
1. Run OPA migration
2. Check permissions on migrated files at destination
ExpectedResult: File permissions match original source permissions

Scenario: Verify OPA migration report shows correct file counts
ExpectedResult: File count in report matches actual files migrated

Scenario: Verify OPA handles network interruption mid-migration gracefully
Steps:
1. Start OPA migration
2. Interrupt network briefly
3. Resume
ExpectedResult: Migration resumes or retries — no data corruption
